subject: Accounting
title: Debits and Credits Explained
description: A quick win: understand Debits and Credits Explained.

hook.text: Debit doesn't mean bad. Credit doesn't mean good. They're just two sides of every transaction.

buildup.text: Debits increase asset and expense accounts. Credits increase liability, equity, and revenue accounts.

discovery.text: When you buy supplies with cash, you debit supplies (up) and credit cash (down). Balance kept.

twist.text: The terms are counterintuitive at first—a bank 'credits' your account, but in your books it's a debit.

climax.text: Once you understand the pattern, every transaction follows the same logic—debit one, credit another.

punchline.text: Debit left. Credit right. Balance always.

quiz.question: What does a debit do to an asset account?
